raw.title: Masonry Lintel + Jamb Design — ASD | TMS 402-22
raw.body_text: Project 💾 Save📂 Load 🖨 Summary📄 Full Calc⊞ Expand All⊝ Collapse Masonry Lintel & Jamb Design — ASD Reinforced CMU Lintel (REK-06) · Out-of-Plane C&C Wind · Reinforced Jamb (REK-04) · TMS 402-22 TMS 402-22 §5.3 & Ch.8 MDG-2022 REK-04 & REK-06 §8.3.2 Flexure · §8.3.5 Shear §8.3.4.2 Combined Axial+Flexure ASCE 7-22 §2.4 ASD — 0.6W factor Print / Export PDF Save Inputs (.json) Load Inputs (.json) Anderson Rohr Engineering — TMS 402-22 ASD DIAGRAMS — LINTEL ELEVATION & SECTION · JAMB ELEVATION & SECTION 30" min. ext. w = 1,200 lb/ft w_OOP = 116 lb/ft (0.6×13 psf) P = 15,026 lb a = 4.33 ft R = 12.7 k R = 12.7 k 8"' clr L = 8.67 ft 8" brg t = 32" d = 28.5" CMU LINTEL BOT. REINF 1-#6 (30" ext. min.) TOP REINF 1-#4 2-#4 JAMB VERT. (EA. JAMB) Lintel — Elevation b = 7.625" t = 32" d = 28.5" TOP REINF 1-#4 2-#5 MIN. EA. COURSE #4 SHEAR REINF (TYP. DETAIL) KNOCK-OUT WEB BOND BEAM BOTTOM REINF 1-#6 Lintel — Section w_OOP = 34 lb/ft (0.6W) P_oop = 462 lb 2-#4 VERT. IN GROUTED CELLS P = R = 12,719 lb H = 18.0 ft H_s = 10.0 ft W = 32" CMU JAMB Jamb — Elevation w_OOP (0.6W) 2-#4 VERT. W = 32" b = 7.625" d = 3.8" Jamb — Section ■ 1. Material Properties CMU Nominal Size 6″ CMU — b = 5.625″ 8″ CMU — b = 7.625″ 12″ CMU — b = 11.625″ b = 7.625″ f′m (psi) 1,500 1,750 2,000 2,500 3,000 Steel Grade Grade 40 — Fs = 20,000 psi Grade 60 — Fs = 32,000 psi Mortar Type (for Ft) Type S Type N Affects OOP flexural tension allowable Ft ■ 2. Opening & Lintel Geometry TMS 402-22 §5.3.1 Clear Opening Span (ft) Bearing Length Each End (in) Min 4″ per §5.3.1.4; 8″ typical Total Lintel Depth t (in) Multiples of 8″ (CMU courses) Effective Depth d (in) Auto = t − 3.5″; adjust if needed Wall Thickness b (in, auto) ■ 3. Wall & Jamb Geometry REK-04 Total Wall Height Hwall (ft) Full wall height; used for wind tributary to lintel Lintel Soffit Height Hsoffit (ft) Floor to bottom of lintel Jamb Width Wjamb (in) Width of masonry pier at each side of opening Jamb Height Hjamb (ft) Full wall height (jamb spans floor to top of wall) Jamb Steel Cover (in) Cover to bar center from OOP wall face. Default 3.81″ = b/2 → centered bars (typical). Reduce only if bars are detailed offset toward the tension face. ■ 4. Lintel Gravity Loads ASD Service Combo D+L Enter floor/roof tributary load only in wtrib. CMU wall self-weight above the opening is computed automatically from the unit weight and geometry (Hwall − Hsoffit). Total lintel load shown below is used in all calculations. wtrib — Floor/Roof Tributary (lb/ft) Floor or roof tributary only — do NOT include wall weight here CMU Unit Weight wwall,psf (psf) Wall weight/ft height incl. grout. Auto-added: wwall = wwall,psf × (Hwall−Hsoffit) wwall — Auto Wall Weight (lb/ft, auto) = wwall,psf × (Hwall − Hsoffit) ft wtotal — Total Lintel Load (lb/ft, auto) = wtrib + wwall — used in all calculations Pconc — Concentrated Load (lb) 0 if none P Location from Left (ft) Auto = midspan wuplift — Uplift Combo (lb/ft) 0.6D combination if net uplift; 0 if N/A Puplift — Uplift Load (lb) ■ 5. Wind Loading — Out-of-Plane C&C Nominal Pressure Input Wind load level: Enter the nominal ASCE 7 strength-level C&C design pressure from the C&C chart (unfactored). The calculator applies the 0.6W ASD factor (ASCE 7-22 §2.4.1, combo 6: D + 0.6W) internally to all wind demands. Note: TMS 402 eliminated the one-third allowable stress increase in the 2011 edition (allowable values are already calibrated for wind/seismic) — leave the checkbox below unchecked unless your jurisdiction's alternative basic load combinations specifically permit it. Nominal C&C Pressure W (psf, strength level) ASCE 7 unfactored design pressure — calc applies 0.6× for ASD ×0.6 → ASD Wind 0.6W (psf, auto) = 0.6 × nominal; used for all demand calculations ×H/2 → Lintel OOP Line Load (lb/ft, auto) = 0.6W × Hwall/2 (trib. to lintel) Apply ⅓ allowable stress increase (multiplies wind-load allowables by 4/3). Not permitted by TMS 402-22 with ASCE 7 §2.4 combinations — the one-third increase was eliminated in 2011. Only check if using IBC alternative basic load combinations that permit it. ■ 6. Lintel Reinforcement Bottom Bars (qty) 1 2 3 Bottom Bar Size #4 — 0.20 in² #5 — 0.31 in² #6 — 0.44 in² #7 — 0.60 in² #8 — 0.79 in² #9 — 1.00 in² Top Bars (qty, uplift) 1 2 3 Top Bar Size #4 — 0.20 in² #5 — 0.31 in² #6 — 0.44 in² #7 — 0.60 in² #8 — 0.79 in² #9 — 1.00 in² Lintel OOP Cover (in) Cover to bar center from OOP face (b direction) — for reinforced OOP tension check when bars are present Shear Reinforcement Bar Size #3 — 0.11 in² #4 — 0.20 in² #5 — 0.31 in² Vertical shear tie/stirrup Av (used only if shear reinforcement is provided) Shear Reinforcement Spacing s (in) Tie spacing per S802 schedule; §8.3.5.2.1 limit = min(d/2, 48″) Provide shear reinforcement in lintel (adds Fvs per Eq. 8-24). Leave unchecked to check the masonry-only shear capacity Fvm (Eq. 8-23); if fv exceeds Fvm the calc will flag that shear reinforcement is required per §8.3.5.2. ■ 7. Jamb Vertical Reinforcement REK-04 Vertical bars resist OOP wind bending. Bars assumed at d = bwall − cover from the tension wall face. Number of Vertical Bars 1 2 3 4 5 6 Typically 1 per 8″ CMU cell across jamb width Jamb Bar Size #4 — 0.20 in² #5 — 0.31 in² #6 — 0.44 in² #7 — 0.60 in² #8 — 0.79 in² #9 — 1.00 in² Bearing Eccentricity e (in) Eccentricity of lintel reaction from jamb centroid (OOP direction). Creates additional OOP bending in jamb. Use 0 if centered or unknown. ▶ Run All Checks ✗ ONE OR MORE CHECKS FAIL Max D/C = 1.424 · f′m = 1750.0 psi · Lclr = 8.0 ft · tlin = 32.0″ · Wjamb = 32.0″ · Wnom = 21.4 psf → 0.6W = 12.8 psf ▮ PART A — Lintel Strong Direction (Gravity) · REK-06 ASD ✗ FAIL — Lintel Gravity — REK-06 · Max D/C = 1.424 ⇄ ResultsJamb axial (gov. lintel rxn + self-wt): 16.32 kips→Lintel left reaction: 12.72 kips→Lintel right reaction: 12.71 kips→Lintel gravity moment: 43.82 kip-ft→Lintel gravity shear: 12.72 kips→Total lintel line load: 1,200 lb/ft 600.0 lb/ft Trib Load w_trib (floor/roof only) 600 lb/ft Wall Weight w_wall auto (75.0 psf×8.0 ft) 1200.0 lb/ft Total w_total (used in all calcs) 43,823 lb·ft Max Gravity Moment M 12,719 lb Max Shear V (at support) 45554.6 psi Steel Stress f_s vs Fs=32000.0 774.1 psi Masonry Stress f_b vs Fb=787.5 12,719 lb Left Reaction R_L → jamb axial 12,707 lb Right Reaction R_R → jamb axial Check Reference Demand Capacity D/C Status Detail Bottom Steel fs As = 0.44 in² ; n = 18.4 ; j = 0.921 TMS 402-22 §8.3.2 fs = 45554.6 psi Fs = 32000.0 psi 1.424 FAIL ▶ Calc Masonry Compression fb k = 0.238 ; j = 0.921 TMS 402-22 §8.3.2.2.1 fb = 774.1 psi Fb = 787.5 psi 0.983 PASS ▶ Calc Shear fv — reinf. required ADD SHEAR REINF per §8.3.5.2 (s≤14.3″) TMS 402-22 §8.3.5 fv = 58.5 psi Fv = 47.1 psi 1.244 FAIL ▶ Calc Min. Bearing Length 8″ typical TMS 402-22 §5.3.1.4 bearing = 8.0 in. ≥ 4.0 in. 0.500 PASS ▶ Calc Deflection Gate Waived: L ≤ 8d TMS 402-22 §5.3.1.6 L/8d = 0.46 ≤ 1.0 (waived) 0.456 Info ▶ Calc Lateral Bracing (Advisory) Continuously braced by wall (typical) TMS 402-22 §5.3.1.3 Lb,max = 244.0 in. — — Info ▶ Calc ρ vs ρ_bal (Advisory) Steel-controlled (ductile) TMS 402-22 §8.3.4.2.2 ρ/ρbal = 0.01 — — Info ▶ Calc ▮ PART B — Lintel Out-of-Plane Wind · 0.6W C&C ✗ FAIL — Lintel Out-of-Plane Wind (0.6W ASD) · Max D/C = 1.036 21.4 psf Nominal C&C W (input, unfactored) 12.8 psf ASD Wind 0.6W (used in calcs) 115.6 lb/ft OOP Line Load 0.6W×H/2 1,085 lb·ft OOP Moment M_oop (weak axis) 42.0 psi OOP Stress vs Fb=583.3 501 lb OOP Shear to Each Jamb 1.036 Biaxial Interaction D/C Check Reference Demand Capacity D/C Status Detail OOP Compressive Stress fb,oop 0.6W = 12.8 psf ; Soop = 310.1 in³ TMS 402-22 §8.2.3 fb,oop = 42.0 psi Fb,oop = 583.3 psi 0.072 PASS ▶ Calc OOP Tension — Reinforced fs,oop Bars present; doop=5.1″ ; k=0.269 ; j=0.910 TMS 402-22 §8.3.2 fs,oop = 6341.9 psi Fs = 32000.0 psi 0.198 PASS ▶ Calc Biaxial Bending Interaction Gravity + OOP at compression corner TMS 402-22 §8.3.2 fb,grav Fb + fb,oop Fb,oop ≤ 1.0 1.036 FAIL ▶ Calc ▮ PART C — Masonry Jamb (Reinforced) · REK-04 ASD ✓ PASS — Masonry Jamb REK-04 · Max D/C = 0.924 16,319 lb Jamb Axial P (lintel rxn + self-wt) 66.9 psi Axial Stress f_a vs Fa=267.1 34.2 lb/ft C&C on Jamb 0.6W×Wj/12 501 lb OOP Pt Load from Lintel 3,395 ft·lb Max Jamb Wind Moment M_j 0.0 psi Jamb Steel f_s (P+M) vs Fs=32000.0 0.329 Combined P+M D/C (cracked sec.) Check Reference Demand Capacity D/C Status Detail Jamb Width ≥ Bearing Geometry: pier ≥ bearing dim. TMS 402-22 §5.3.1.4 Bearing = 8.0 in. Wjamb = 32.0 in. 0.250 PASS ▶ Calc Bearing Stress fbrg Abrg = 61.0 in² TMS 402-22 §8.1.6 fbrg = 208.5 psi Fbr = 577.5 psi 0.361 PASS ▶ Calc Axial Stress fa h/r = 98.1 ; r = 2.20 in. TMS 402-22 §8.3.4 fa = 66.9 psi Fa = 267.1 psi 0.250 PASS ▶ Calc Jamb OOP Steel fs,j Mj = 3,395 ft·lb ; 0.6W applied TMS 402-22 §8.3.2 fs,j = 29582.6 psi Fs = 32000.0 psi 0.924 PASS ▶ Calc Jamb OOP Masonry fb,j bj = 32.0 in ; d = 3.81 in TMS 402-22 §8.3.2.2.1 fb,j = 663.4 psi Fb = 787.5 psi 0.842 PASS ▶ Calc Slender Stability Cap P≤¼Pe h/r = 98.1 ; e = 0.00″ TMS 402-22 Eq. 8-10 P = 16,319 lb ¼Pe = 98,469 lb 0.166 PASS ▶ Calc Combined P+M (Cracked Section) Cracked ; c=3.92″ TMS 402-22 §8.3.4.2.2 fb=259.2 / fs=0.0 psi 0.45f′m / Fs 0.329 PASS ▶ Calc Engineering Notes: Design per TMS 402-22 ASD / MDG-2022 REK-04 (jamb) and REK-06 (lintel). Enter gravity loads per ASCE 7-22 §2.4 ASD combinations. Wind convention: Enter the nominal ASCE 7 strength-level C&C pressure from the design chart. The calculator multiplies by 0.6 (ASCE 7-22 §2.4.1, combo D + 0.6W) for all wind demands. TMS 402 eliminated the one-third allowable stress increase in 2011 — allowables (including Table 8.2.4.2 flexural tension) are already calibrated for wind/seismic, so no further increase should be applied with §2.4 combinations. Effective lintel span: L = clear + bearing/12 per §5.3.1.1. Minimum bearing 4″ is enforced as a check (Part A) per §5.3.1.4; 8″ typical. Both the lintel gravity moment/shear AND the lintel out-of-plane wind moment are computed over this effective span L. Lintel shear (§8.3.5): fv = V/Anv (Eq. 8-19). Masonry allowable Fvm per Eq. 8-23 (including the +0.25 P/An term; P = 0 on the lintel). When "provide shear reinforcement" is enabled, Fvs = 0.5⋅AvFsdv/(Anvs) is added (Eq. 8-24) and Fv = (Fvm+Fvs)γg capped by γg(2–3)√f′m (Eq. 8-20/8-21/8-22). Lintel modeled fully grouted → γg = 1.0. If fv exceeds Fvm with no reinforcement provided, the calc flags "shear reinforcement required per §8.3.5.2" (max spacing min(d/2, 48″), §8.3.5.2.1). Shear is taken at the support (conservative; §8.3.5 permits d/2). Deflection (§5.3.1.6): deflection need not be checked when L ≤ 8d (§5.3.1.6.1; immediate deflection ≈ L/600). When L > 8d the calc emits a WARNING that a manual Ieff evaluation is required per §5.3.1.6.2 (Eq. 5-1), with the cracking moment Mcr computed from the Table 8.2.4.2 allowable flexural tensile stress × 2.5 and the deflection limited to L/600 (§4.6). Integral-wall end restraint (20–45% reduction, commentary) is not automatically credited. Lintel OOP line load: Woop = 0.6W × Hwall/2. OOP section modulus Soop = tlin×b²/6 (bending about vertical axis). When lintel bars are present, OOP tension is checked as a reinforced section (steel stress check, doop = b − OOP cover). When no bars are present, the unreinforced flexural tensile limit Ft from Table 8.2.4.2 governs. Jamb OOP model: simply supported vertical beam with (1) uniform C&C = 0.6W×Wj/12 distributed load, and (2) lintel OOP reaction as point load at mid-lintel height. Zero-shear method locates max moment. Jamb axial demand = governing lintel reaction + jamb tributary self-weight Psw = wwall,psf × Hjamb × (Wjamb/12) to the base section. Bearing stress (§8.1.6) uses only the lintel reaction (self-weight acts below the bearing plane). Allowable axial Fa per Eq. 8-16/8-17 (0.30 f′m; the 0.65 AstFs steel term is conservatively neglected). Jamb slender-column stability (Eq. 8-10): P ≤ ¼Pe, with Pe = π²EmIn/h² × (1 − 0.577 e/r)³ (Eq. 8-14), Em = 900 f′m, In = Wjambb³/12, h = Hjamb×12 (pin-pin), e = bearing eccentricity. The cubic term is clamped ≥ 0 and the calc flags when e/r exceeds the Eq. 8-11 validity range (e > r/0.577). Jamb combined axial + flexure (§8.3.4.2.2): solved by a linear-elastic cracked-section P+M analysis (transformed section Wjamb×b, single steel layer at d, modular ratio n) — the neutral-axis depth c is found from P+M equilibrium (no stress superposition). fb ≤ 0.45 f′m and fs ≤ Fs. When P+M leaves no net tension the gross (uncracked) section is used. No moment magnifier is applied — TMS ASD credits the axial-load safety factor (§8.3.3 commentary) and limits stability via Eq. 8-10. The unreinforced interaction Eq. 8-9 / "Eq. 8-20" is NOT used (per commentary it does not apply to reinforced masonry; Eq. 8-20 is the shear equation). Bearing eccentricity e (in): the lintel reaction acts eccentric from the jamb centroid (OOP direction), adding Mecc = Rlintel × e. This is combined with the OOP wind moment in the cracked-section P+M solve above. Enter 0 if the load is centered. Jamb effective depth d = bwall − cover. Default cover 3.81″ = b/2 assumes centered-cell bars (d = b/2, typical). If d exceeds b/2 the calc flags that offset bars are assumed — verify the placement is detailed and enforceable. No 1/3 stress increase applied (consistent with TMS 402-22, which eliminated the increase in 2011). Wall self-weight is automatically computed from wwall,psf × (Hwall − Hsoffit) and added to the floor/roof tributary load. Enter only the floor/roof tributary load in wtrib. Total wtotal = wtrib + wwall is used for all moment, shear, and reaction calculations. Print button generates a clean printout/PDF of all inputs and results. Save/Load buttons store and retrieve input sets as .json files for different opening configurations. ⇄ Results Hub ⇄ Results Hub× Total lintel line load1,200 lb/ft Masonry Lintel + Jamb Design — ASD | TMS 402-22 · 7/11/2026 Copy Lintel gravity shear12.72 kips Masonry Lintel + Jamb Design — ASD | TMS 402-22 · 7/11/2026 Copy Lintel gravity moment43.82 kip-ft Masonry Lintel + Jamb Design — ASD | TMS 402-22 · 7/11/2026 Copy Lintel right reaction12.71 kips Masonry Lintel + Jamb Design — ASD | TMS 402-22 · 7/11/2026 Copy Lintel left reaction12.72 kips Masonry Lintel + Jamb Design — ASD | TMS 402-22 · 7/11/2026 Copy Jamb axial (gov. lintel rxn + self-wt)16.32 kips Masonry Lintel + Jamb Design — ASD | TMS 402-22 · 7/11/2026 Copy Max D/C ratio0.938 Masonry Lintel Design — ASD · 7/11/2026 Copy Lintel line load519 lb/ft Masonry Lintel Design — ASD · 7/11/2026 ⤓ Use here (w_load)Copy Right reaction11.46 kips Masonry Lintel Design — ASD · 7/11/2026 Copy Left reaction11.47 kips Masonry Lintel Design — ASD · 7/11/2026 Copy Lintel max shear11.47 kips Masonry Lintel Design — ASD · 7/11/2026 Copy Lintel max moment53.75 kip-ft Masonry Lintel Design — ASD · 7/11/2026 Copy Max D/C ratio1.037 Masonry ASD Design Calculator · 7/11/2026 Copy Required steel area0.415 in^2 Masonry ASD Design Calculator · 7/11/2026 Copy Allowable shear stress47.06 psi Masonry ASD Design Calculator · 7/11/2026 Copy Design shear0.964 kips Masonry ASD Design Calculator · 7/11/2026 Copy Design moment3.807 kip-ft Masonry ASD Design Calculator · 7/11/2026 Copy Anchor T+V Interaction0 Masonry Top-of-Wall Anchor — ASD | TMS 402-22 §8.1.4.3 · 7/11/2026 Copy Anchor Allow. Shear Bv3.162 kips Masonry Top-of-Wall Anchor — ASD | TMS 402-22 §8.1.4.3 · 7/11/2026 Copy Anchor Allow. Tension Ba2.662 kips Masonry Top-of-Wall Anchor — ASD | TMS 402-22 §8.1.4.3 · 7/11/2026 Copy

elevations do not show recognizable individual block face shells/webs
audit.dimensions: PASS for driven span, bearing, wall thickness, height and cover labels
